data.acceptance:
1) When terminal is running (expanded), users can scroll from the bottom to the first item in the Queue/Active list without being blocked by an artificial upper bound.
2) When terminal is minimized, users can still scroll to the same true top boundary of the list; the maximum upward scroll is not shortened by minimized-terminal layout state.
3) Switching terminal state between running/ minimized/closed updates scroll bounds immediately without refresh and preserves expected scroll-to-top behavior in each state.
4) Validate in-browser with Playwright and/or Chrome DevTools: assert `scrollTop`, `scrollHeight`, and `clientHeight` in each terminal state and confirm the computed scroll range reaches the expected top limit in both running and minimized states.
